title: Array Stats Reporter
prompt_md:
## Goal
Read integers into a `std::array`, then report its length and specific elements using both `operator[]` and `.at()`.

## Input
- A single line containing exactly 5 space-separated integers.

## Output
- Line 1: `Size: N` where N is the result of calling `.size()` on the array.
- Line 2: `First: X` where X is the first element, accessed with `operator[]`.
- Line 3: `Last: X` where X is the last element, accessed with `.at()`.
- Line 4: `Middle: X` where X is the element at index 2, accessed with `.at()`.

## Requirements
1. Declare a `std::array<int, 5>` and read all 5 values from stdin into it using `operator[]` in a loop.
2. Use `.size()` to print the array's length -- do not hard-code `5` in the output statement.
3. Access the first element with `operator[]` and the last element with `.at()`. Use `.size() - 1` to compute the last index.
4. Access the middle element (index 2) with `.at()`.
5. Include `<array>`, `<iostream>`, and `<cstddef>`.

title: Safe Element Lookup
prompt_md:
## Goal
Build a program that reads an array and an index, then uses `.at()` to safely look up the element -- catching the exception if the index is out of range.

## Input
- Line 1: exactly 6 space-separated integers (the array elements).
- Line 2: a single integer representing the index to look up.

## Output
- Line 1: `Size: N` where N is the array's `.size()`.
- Line 2: one of the following:
  - `Value: X` if the index is valid, where X is the element at that index.
  - `Error: index out of range` if `.at()` throws an exception.

## Requirements
1. Declare a `std::array<int, 6>` and read all 6 values from stdin.
2. Read the lookup index into an `int`, then cast it to `std::size_t`.
3. Use a `try`/`catch` block around the `.at()` call. Catch `const std::out_of_range&`.
4. Print the size on line 1 regardless of whether the lookup succeeds.
5. On success, print `Value: X`. On failure, print `Error: index out of range`.
6. Include `<array>`, `<iostream>`, `<cstddef>`, and `<stdexcept>`.

test_cases.label: Hidden -- out of range index 10
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Size: 6
Error: index out of range
